data.title: Spire Inc. (NYSE: SR) declares $0.785 quarterly dividend per share, payable on Oct 2, 2025 to shareholders.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, the board of directors of Spire Inc. (NYSE: SR) announced a quarterly common stock dividend of $0.785 per share. The dividend is set to be paid on October 2, 2025, to shareholders who are recorded as such on September 11, 2025. Spire has a long-standing history of issuing cash dividends, dating back to 1946. This signifies a consistent commitment to rewarding shareholders, and reflects the company's stable financial performance.

data.title: FirstService Residential chosen to manage Selene Oceanfront Residences in Ft. Lauderdale high-rise. Leading property management company.
data.description: FirstService Residential, the top property management company in North America, has been chosen to provide property management and lifestyle services for a new high-rise building in Ft. Lauderdale. The announcement was made on July 31, 2025. This new project adds to the company's portfolio, showcasing its expertise in managing properties. The high-rise building in Ft. Lauderdale will benefit from FirstService Residential's experience and commitment to service excellence.

data.title: Dow Jones rises on nearing China trade deal. Arm plunges on earnings. Core inflation exceeds. Meta, Carvana enter.
data.description: On Thursday, the Dow Jones index saw an increase as signs pointed to a potential trade agreement with China. However, Arm, a partner of Nvidia, experienced a significant decrease in earnings. The stock market today showed that indexes were reducing their gains as core inflation numbers surpassed expectations. Additionally, companies like Meta and Carvana made new entries in the market.
U.S. GASOLINE DEMAND AVERAGED 9.06 MILLION BARRELS PER DAY IN MAY, MARKING THE LOWEST LEVEL FOR THE MONTH SINCE 2020, ACCORDING TO THE EIA.
U.S. DEMAND FOR CRUDE OIL AND PETROLEUM PRODUCTS, AS MEASURED BY PRODUCT SUPPLIED, ROSE IN MAY TO ITS HIGHEST LEVEL SINCE JANUARY, ACCORDING TO THE EIA.

data.title: Check Point Software reported Q2 earnings of $2.37/share and sales of $665.2 million, beating analyst estimates. CEO optimistic about growth and recent acquisition.
data.description: Checkpoint Software Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ: CHKP) released positive second-quarter earnings on Wednesday. The company exceeded analyst expectations by reporting earnings of $2.37 per share compared to the estimated $2.36 per share. Quarterly sales also surpassed predictions, reaching $665.200 million as opposed to the expected $661.751 million. CEO Nadav Zafrir attributed this success to growth in emerging technologies such as Email, SASE, and Enterprise Risk Management. The Quantum Force appliances experienced double-digit growth, reflecting customer confidence in Check Point's innovative and prevention-focused approach. Zafrir highlighted the recent acquisition of Veriti, which bolsters the Infinity platform's architecture. The company's outlook for the third quarter is promising based on strong performance indicators in July and a robust pipeline of opportunities.

data.title: Old Dominion Freight Line (NASDAQ: ODFL) reports Q2 earnings down to $1.27/share from $1.48 last year, revenue fell 6.1% to $1.41 billion, missing expectations. CEO attributes downturn to soft domestic economy. Market share remains steady.
data.description: Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. (NASDAQ: ODFL) announced disappointing second-quarter results with earnings per share at $1.27, a 14.2% decrease from the previous year's $1.48, falling short of the projected $1.30. Revenue also declined by 6.1% to $1.41 billion, missing the expected $1.43 billion. Marty Freeman, the company's President and CEO, attributed the weak performance to the sluggish domestic economy. Despite these challenges, Old Dominion maintained its market share and continued to implement its strategic plan focused on providing exceptional value to customers. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: QuantiPath.bio hires 2 new executives ahead of GenoPATHx launch. Michael Connolly as General Counsel and Janine Williams.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, a biotechnology company called QuantiPath.bio made an official announcement regarding their latest executive team expansion. The company, known for revolutionizing food safety with precise genetic diagnostics, welcomed two notable professionals to their leadership. Michael Connolly joined as the General Counsel while Janine Williams assumed a prominent role within the team.
